results.snippet: Cloudscape uses a 4px base unit where compact mode reduces the spacing scale in increments of 4, decreasing vertical spacing inside components and between them. Documents the two-mode (comfortable/compact) system used in production AWS UIs — directly applicable to a mod card browser needing a density toggle that scales card padding, gap, and font size via CSS custom properties.
results.title: Content Density — Cloudscape Design System (AWS)

results.snippet: Documents compact/normal/comfortable density toggling with instant row-height changes. The underlying pattern — a three-state toggle that mutates a single density prop and lets CSS cascade the rest — translates directly to card grids: swap `data-density` attribute on the container, drive `--card-gap`, `--card-padding`, `--card-min-width` from CSS custom properties.
results.title: Density Toggle Guide — Material React Table V3

results.snippet: Explains the data-attribute + CSS custom property approach: set `[data-ui-density]` on a root element, then write spacing as `calc(.5em * var(--ui-density))`. This is the cleanest CSS-only architecture for a density toggle — no JS layout recalculation, no class churn, just one attribute flip that cascades through all 291 mod cards simultaneously.
results.title: Designing for Display Density — Bobby Showalter

results.snippet: Carbon's content switcher covers the button-group UI for binary or multi-state view toggling (grid/list, compact/comfortable). Provides three height variants (32/40/48px) and the accessibility contract: switcher must act immediately with no submit step, each state must have a visible label or icon, and the active state must be visually distinct. Covers exactly the toggle button UX for the view-mode switcher control above the 291-card grid.
results.title: Content Switcher Usage — IBM Carbon Design System

results.snippet: PatternFly's card view pattern documents grid-of-cards-as-gallery UX, including when to offer a card↔table toggle, how to structure card anatomy (header badge, body text, footer action), and density considerations. Directly maps to the CoH mod browser use case: name, category badge, download count, description, and a download CTA button all fit the PatternFly card anatomy.
results.title: Card View Design Guidelines — PatternFly

results.snippet: Real mod-browser user feedback thread requesting a CurseForge-style tile/grid toggle for Modrinth. Captures authentic user expectations: players want grid for visual browsing of 100s of mods, list for reading descriptions efficiently, and the toggle to persist across sessions. Useful signal for which density modes matter most to mod-browser audiences specifically.
results.title: Tiles or Grid Feature to View Mods and Library — Modrinth GitHub Issue
